About: It is about planetary empire building, economics, and diplomacy.
Affects: It affects the player's tax revenues.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with planetary systems.
ConsistsOf: It consists of two levels of play: Basic and Advanced.
DevelopedBy: It was developed by Steve Jackson.
HasA: It has enough counters for eight players in the Metagaming version.
It has enough counters for four players in the Task Force Games version.
It has four states on each planet that are bitter rivals.
It has embassies on other players' planets.
It has a two-piece map.
It has a booklet of 15 system sheets.
It has six National Government sheets.
It has a player reference card.
It has 960 die-cut counters.
It has 616 paper Gigabuck money chits.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by planetary empire building.
It is characterized by interplanetary coalitions.
It is characterized by political rules.
It is characterized by internal dissent during interplanetary warfare.
IsA: It is a science fiction board wargame.
It is a game for up to 15 players.
It is a ziplock bag game.
LocatedIn: It is located in the open star cluster Narym.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the open star cluster Narym.
PartOf: It is part of the open star cluster Narym.
It is part of planetary systems.
ProducedBy: It was produced by Metagaming Concepts.
It was produced by Task Force Games.
PublishedAs: It was published as a ziplock bag game.
It was published as a boxed set.
PublishedBy: It was published by Metagaming Concepts.
It was published by Task Force Games.
UsedFor: It is used for simulating planetary empire building.
It is used for simulating economics.
It is used for simulating diplomacy.
It is used for encouraging revolt and a change in government.
It is used for building up defense.
It is used for going on the attack against a neighboring system.
It is used for building the local economy.
It is used for subverting someone else's government.
UsedIn: It is used in the open star cluster Narym.
WrittenBy: It was written by Lynn Willis.
